com.mentor.chs.plugin.authentication

Interface IXClientLogin

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXBaseLogin, IXPlugin

    public interface IXClientLogin
extends IXBaseLogin
    This interface is to be implemented if pluggable authentication(authentication outside of Capital) is desired for the Capital applications. This interface should be implemented if custom client side authentication is required.

    Capital client will delegate the user authentication responsibility to the plugin and will pass the response to Capital Manager. When a user enters the user name and password for logging into Capital Client Applications , the object implementating this inteface will be automatically called from capital client passing the user name and password to the authenticate method. Inturn the implementation should return an implementation of IXClientAuthenticationResponse indicating if the authentication is successful or not.

    WARNING: When implementing plugins NEVER NEVER EVER exit the application. 

        System.exit(); // This is BAD

    This will cause the application to exit in a very untidy way and will result in the loss of any unsaved data. DO NOT DO THIS.

    See Also:
    IXClientAuthenticationResponse

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      IXClientAuthenticationResponse authenticate(java.lang.String username, java.lang.String password)
      Authenticate the given user, with the provided username and password.

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.plugin.IXPlugin

        getDescription, getName, getVersion

    • Method Detail

      • authenticate

        IXClientAuthenticationResponse authenticate(java.lang.String username,
                                            java.lang.String password)
        Authenticate the given user, with the provided username and password.

        Authenticate a user with the given username and password and return the IXClientAuthenticationResponse object indicating if Authentication is successful or not. If it returns null then it willl indicate authentication failed.

        This custom plugin is used by all client applications.

        NOTE: There should be only one plugin of this type available in the client install. Incase there are more than one plugin then authentication will fail and user will not be able to login in into capital Client Applications

        Specified by:
        authenticate in interface IXBaseLogin
        Parameters:
        username - user name. It will carry the identifier in case of manager plugin if client authentication strategy is used at the client side
        password - password. It will carry the ticket in case of manager plugin if client authentication strategy is used at the client side.
        Returns:
        the authenticate response.
